a204-1.description: s1817 incomplete B session — recorded as 'session started, awaiting completion' with no duration, cost, or file edits. Caused false-positive pipeline gate violation in audit-stats.
a204-1.resolution: UNRESOLVED. A#204 recommended creating wq-883 for incomplete-session detection. However, wq-883 ID was consumed by a brainstorming auto-promote (BRIEFING.md auto-staleness detection), which was subsequently retired as duplicate of wq-863. The actual incomplete-session detection recommendation was never implemented. R#324 (s1823) attempted to retire wq-883 but it was the wrong item. The s1817 anomaly remains a one-time occurrence with no recurrence since, but the detection gap persists.
a204-1.metric_after: Still no incomplete-session detection — wq ID collision with auto-promote
a204-1.stale_note: 2nd audit without progress. ID collision between audit recommendation and brainstorming auto-promote is the root cause. Escalating to new wq item with explicit title.

a204-2.description: d073 deliverable (4) mathematically impossible within remaining 13 sessions — requires 20 consecutive zero-manual R sessions but only ~3 R sessions remain before s1833 deadline.
a204-2.resolution: ACKNOWLEDGED. No R session since A#204 has addressed d073 partial completion. R#324 (s1823) focused on audit subcheck slimming instead. d073 now has 8 sessions remaining (deadline s1833). Only ~1-2 R sessions remain. Deliverable (4) remains mathematically impossible. Recommend R session close d073 as partial success at deadline.
a204-2.metric_after: 8 sessions remaining, deliverable (4) still infeasible, no action taken
